Short Description: System Architecture Design: Defined 4-workflow orchestration pattern (Intake -> Classifier -> Responder + Error Handler). Designed webhook event flow from Microsoft Teams Graph API subscriptions. Established data contracts between workflows including message_id, chat_id, intent, urgency, summary, requires_task fields. Documented state management approach using Supabase processed_messages table with status tracking (processing -> classified -> replied).

Supabase Database Schema Creation: Created processed_messages table with columns: message_id (PK), chat_id, status, intent, urgency, summary, requires_task, sender_name, processed_at, classified_at, replied_at. Created error_log table with: workflow, node_name, message_id, error_message, payload, timestamp. Configured Row Level Security policies and service role access for n8n integration. Added indexes on message_id and status fields for query optimization.

Microsoft Graph API OAuth2 Configuration: Registered application in Azure AD. Configured Microsoft OAuth2 API credential in n8n with client_id, client_secret, tenant_id. Granted permissions: Chat.Read, ChatMessage.Read, ChatMessage.Send. Validated token refresh flow. Tested Graph API endpoints: GET /me/chats/{chatId}/messages/{messageId} and POST /me/chats/{chatId}/messages for reply functionality.

PEC-Intake Workflow Development: Built webhook trigger node at path 'teams-events' accepting POST requests. Implemented validation token response for Microsoft Graph subscription verification. Created resource parser to extract chatId and messageId from notification resource URLs (regex patterns for /chats/{id}/messages/{id} and chats('id')/messages('id') formats). Added conditional logic to handle validation vs. actual events. Connected webhook response nodes for proper HTTP 200 acknowledgment.

Atomic Claim Pattern Implementation: Developed deduplication logic using Supabase POST to processed_messages table with Prefer: resolution=ignore-duplicates header. Implemented 201 vs 409 status code checking to determine if message was claimed. Added If: Claimed? conditional to route only unprocessed messages to classifier. Prevents race conditions when multiple webhook deliveries occur for same message. Includes error handling for network failures.

PEC-Classifier Workflow - Message Fetch & Clean: Built HTTP Request node to call Microsoft Graph API with chatId and messageId from trigger. Implemented Handle Fetch Failure code node with fallback response for unavailable messages. Created Strip HTML code node to decode HTML entities (< > & ") and remove all HTML tags using regex. Added whitespace normalization. Output provides cleanedContent field for LLM processing.

AI Classification Prompt Engineering: Designed comprehensive classification prompt with 9 intent categories: password_reset, software_issue, hardware_issue, access_request, data_engineering, business_reports, business_intelligence, ai_initiatives, general_inquiry. Defined 4 urgency levels (critical/high/medium/low) with specific criteria. Added response_tone mapping (urgent/professional/friendly). Created requires_task boolean logic. Included 5 detailed examples covering different scenarios. Enforced strict JSON output format validation.

OpenAI Integration & Agent Configuration: Configured OpenAI Chat Model node (GPT-5.4-mini) with temperature 0.1 for consistent outputs. Set up Agent Memory node with sessionKey mapped to chatId for conversation context. Implemented Structured Output Parser with JSON schema defining intent, urgency, summary, requires_task, response_tone fields. Configured LangChain agent with openAiFunctionsAgent type. Added retry logic (3 attempts with 5s delay) for API resilience.

PEC-Responder Workflow - Dynamic Reply Generation: Built Execute Workflow Trigger receiving chatId, messageId, intent, urgency, summary, requires_task, response_tone. Created urgency-based message templates: critical='URGENT: specialist being alerted immediately', high='escalated - response within 30 min', medium='assigned - response within 1 hour', low='logged - response within 2 hours'. Implemented Build Reply Message code node to construct Teams API request body with proper JSON formatting and signature suffix.

Bot Message Loop Prevention: Implemented Is From Bot? conditional node checking if message body contains '(Automated response from PEC Assist)' signature. Created Update Status: Bot Skip Supabase node to mark bot messages as replied with intent='bot_message'. Added Stop: From Bot noOp terminal node. Prevents infinite reply loops when the bot receives its own messages from Teams webhook. Essential for production stability.

PEC-Error-Handler Workflow Development: Configured Error Trigger node to capture execution failures from linked workflows. Built Extract Error Details code node parsing execution data to extract: workflow name, lastNodeExecuted, error message, messageId from nodeExecutionData traversal. Created Log Error to Supabase node inserting to error_log table. Added Success terminal node. Handles 4 workflows total: PEC-Intake, PEC-Classifier, PEC-Responder, and PEC-Error-Handler itself.

Workflow Error Routing Configuration: Set errorWorkflow setting to 'gZxrCH09fh6NK7CU' (PEC-Error-Handler) in all 3 main workflow settings. Configured callerPolicy to 'workflowsFromSameOwner' for security. Enabled executionOrder v1 for consistent node execution. Applied retryOnFail settings with maxTries=3 and waitBetweenTries=2000ms on external API calls (Microsoft Graph, OpenAI). Verified error propagation chain through manual failure testing.

End-to-End Integration Testing: Validated complete flow: 1) Teams message sends webhook to PEC-Intake, 2) Atomic claim succeeds (201), 3) PEC-Classifier fetches message via Graph API, 4) HTML stripped successfully, 5) OpenAI classifies intent/urgency correctly, 6) Supabase updated with classification data, 7) PEC-Responder generates appropriate reply based on urgency, 8) Teams message sent via Graph API, 9) Supabase status updated to 'replied'. Tested edge cases: duplicate messages, bot messages, fetch failures.
